title: Bitcoin Stamps Protocols
description: Rules for understanding and working with Bitcoin Stamps protocols specifications.

patterns.description: Comprehensive protocol specifications for all supported formats
instructions: This is the authoritative source for protocol specifications.
Includes specifications for Classic Stamps, SRC-20, SRC-721, SRC-721r, SRC-101, and OLGA.
Details transaction sources (direct Bitcoin and Counterparty) and their handling.
Refer to this document when implementing protocol validation or making changes.

patterns.description: Implementation of stamp validation and processing
instructions: Handles stamp data extraction and validation.
Implements dual-source architecture (direct Bitcoin and Counterparty).
Base64 decoding and repair functions for stamp images.
Follow existing patterns when adding new features.
Implements deploy, mint, and transfer operations.
Only direct Bitcoin SRC-20 transactions are processed after block 796000.
Implements domain registration, transfer, and renewal.
Validates domain names according to protocol rules.

patterns.description: Rust implementation for efficient transaction parsing
instructions: High-performance pattern matching for stamp protocols.
Used by the Transaction Filter for efficient filtering.
Detects PREFIX and validates transaction structure.

conceptDefinitions.definition: Original Bitcoin Stamps format for immutable image storage on the Bitcoin blockchain.
Fungible token protocol inspired by BRC-20 but with the immutability guarantees of Stamps.
Non-fungible token protocol using JSON manifests to construct complex, layered images.
Recursive NFT protocol extending SRC-721 with JavaScript and other libraries for complex compositions.
Domain name system native to Bitcoin Stamps.
On-chain Lightweight Graphics Array format that eliminates Base64 encoding overhead, using P2WSH outputs.
Architecture supporting both direct Bitcoin transactions and Counterparty-based transactions.
Bitcoin Stamps can originate from direct Bitcoin transactions or Counterparty transactions.
The 'stamp:' prefix used to identify protocol-relevant data in transactions.

relatedDocumentation.description: System architecture including transaction processing pipeline
Database schema for storing protocol data
Documentation for the Rust parser component
